scenario: Mastodon work session - audit the Status contract before a teardown rework

map_quality.rationale: Hands the agent a usable Rails map: names Mastodon as federated ActivityPub, lists every relevant directory (models/services/workers/lib/controllers/policies/serializers/presenters), and locates Status at app/models/status.rb:34 as the hub. Agent can begin with small targeted lookups.

specificity.rationale: Paths are concrete: app/models/status.rb:34, app/models/status.rb:35-46, app/lib/feed_manager.rb, app/lib/StatusReachFinder, ActivityPub::*, Vacuum::*, app/controllers/api/v1, lib/mastodon/cli — not vague layer names.

justification.rationale: Explains why Status is central ('Everything users post is a Status; feeds, notifications, federation, moderation, search and trends all hang off it') and that concerns supply shared behavior. Could be deeper on WHY the split exists, but the motivation is present.

uncertainty.rationale: No explicit flagging of unknowns or layers it could not confirm; treats the map as fully resolved. Given the brevity and the fast wall time, some hedging on how layers connect would have been appropriate.

relationship.rationale: States connecting relationships: Status belongs_to Account (status.rb:77), 10 concerns are included at status.rb:35-46, services call via .new.call, workers do async fan-out, lib holds FeedManager/StatusReachFinder/ActivityPub. Doesn't yet trace feeds→statuses or federation→statuses paths explicitly, but the routing of behavior into the model is named.

steps.step: Map the Status contract and what it is built on

map_quality.rationale: Maps the model, every concern with file:line, the full association teardown surface grouped by dependent strategy, lifecycle callbacks, and teardown methods — the agent can change the contract with only small targeted lookups.

specificity.rationale: Every piece is named with concrete file:line (e.g., `app/models/status.rb:34`, `:178`, `safe_reblog_insert.rb:3`) and the dependent strategies are enumerated individually rather than 'the associations'.

justification.rationale: Explains WHY several pieces matter (`default_scope { recent.kept }` is 'teardown-critical', `prepend: true` 'must run before dependent: :destroy', SafeReblogInsert blocks reblogs of discarded at SQL level), tying shape to behavior.

uncertainty.rationale: No explicit flagging of any unconfirmed piece of the contract; the answer reads as fully confident throughout.

relationship.rationale: Each concern is bound to Status via 'what it adds to the contract' (Threading supplies ancestors/descendants SQL, Visibility supplies the enum + scopes, SnapshotConcern brings has_many :edits), and associations are tied by dependent strategy — the connecting relation is stated, not just the file.

steps.step: Trace how a status is created and distributed

map_quality.rationale: Traces the full chain post→service→persist→fan-out (feeds, notifications, federation) with file:line for each layer (`PostStatusService:42`, `DistributionWorker:8`, `FanOutOnWriteService:12`, `ActivityPub::DistributionWorker:11`) and explicitly labels inline vs async at each step, leaving only small targeted lookups.

specificity.rationale: Exact files and line numbers throughout (e.g. `app/services/fan_out_on_write_service.rb:12`, `:78,83,102` for `LocalNotificationWorker`, `:116,124,132` for `FeedInsertWorker`, `feed_insert_worker.rb:61-77`); no vague references.

justification.rationale: Explains the layering (inline persistence, async fan-out, per-visibility delivery) but doesn't explicitly call out WHY distribution is pushed async (fan-out scale / latency); the rationale is implicit in the inline/async labels.

uncertainty.rationale: Marks inline vs async clearly but doesn't flag any place it couldn't fully open (e.g. the federation delivery internals after `ActivityPub::DistributionWorker`); no explicit hedges.

relationship.rationale: Presents creation as an explicit chain — PostStatusService → process_status! → save → postprocess_status! → DistributionWorker → FanOutOnWriteService → FeedInsertWorker/LocalNotificationWorker plus ActivityPub::DistributionWorker, with the remote inbound path looping back to the same fan-out — rather than a flat file list.

steps.step: Audit every dependent of the Status contract

map_quality.rationale: Exhaustive enumeration grouped by exactly the requested areas (feeds, federation, notifications, moderation, lifecycle, import/export & backup, maintenance, plus an 'other entry points' group), each item with file:line and a one-phrase usage note. Hits the non-obvious targets (BackupService, AnnualReportsPresenter, PermalinkRedirector, FeedManager, Vacuum, FASP concern) the prompt called out.

specificity.rationale: Nearly every dependent is named by file:line and symbol (e.g. `app/services/backup_service.rb:154`, `app/lib/permalink_redirector.rb:11`, `app/models/concerns/account/merging.rb:6`) with a concrete phrase on how it touches Status; no 'various services' filler.

justification.rationale: Each dependent gets a short why-it-matters phrase ('reassigns statuses on account merge', 'user archive export', 'insert/remove statuses in Redis feeds'), and the area grouping itself acts as a rationale. Could go deeper on a few items but the grouping carries the justification well.

uncertainty.rationale: Header cites tool provenance (sense_graph 124 callers, sense_blast 322/108 files) which is some calibration, but the answer never flags what a static pass might miss (e.g. dependents reached via polymorphic `activity`, derived-from-status routes) — the rubric explicitly asks for that acknowledgment.

relationship.rationale: Many entries state the connecting relation explicitly: `Notification#target_status` flagged as 'polymorphic activity', `Account#statuses` via `belongs_to :account, inverse_of: :statuses`, `MediaAttachment (belongs_to status)`, FASP via concern path, BackupService via favourite/bookmark derivation. Some scattered dependents (timeline controllers, serializers) are listed as endpoints without the path, keeping it from a top score.

steps.step: Trace how status identity and visibility are guarded

map_quality.rationale: Provides a clear three-layer map (existence, kept/discarded, visibility/auth) with file:line across workers (`DistributionWorker:10-12`, `FeedInsertWorker:24-25`), the `default_scope { recent.kept }` at `status.rb:128`, `Status::Visibility#distributable?` and `StatusPolicy#show?`. The agent could re-confirm with small lookups.

specificity.rationale: Nearly every guard is cited with a concrete file and line: `safe_reblog_insert.rb:20`, `status_reach_finder.rb:70/88/104/114`, `visibility.rb:31`, `status_policy.rb:4`, `fan_out_on_write_service.rb:17,29,188`. No vague language.

justification.rationale: Explains WHY each layer exists: default_scope hides soft-deleted rows, `SafeReblogInsert` refuses reblogs of discarded rows, `check_race_condition!` re-queues a half-built status, `proper` resolves reblogs to the original. The motivation is stated, not just the names.

uncertainty.rationale: Does not flag where a guard might be missing or only implicit (e.g., backup paths, moderation handlers) — the answer reads as exhaustive without hedging.

relationship.rationale: Most guards are tied to the dependent they protect: `distributable?` is linked to `FanOutOnWriteService#broadcastable?`, counter caches, `StatusReachFinder`, and `should_fetch_replies?`; `StatusFilter` is linked to `ThreadingConcern` and `FeedManager#filter`. The path from guard to consumer is explicit, not just a method list.

steps.step: Assess the blast radius of the contract change

map_quality.rationale: Comprehensive risk-ranked blast radius across every area (teardown, federation, feeds, search, cleanup, moderation, backup, serializers) with file:line and concrete reasons; opens with sense_blast totals (322 symbols/108 files). Agent has a complete tiered map to act on.

specificity.rationale: Dependents named with exact file:line (e.g., `app/services/remove_status_service.rb:16`, `status.rb:87-118`, `FeedManager#clear_from_home/list :231,251`) and a short reason per risk level.

justification.rationale: Explicitly grounds risk in the contract surface — `bypasses default_scope`, `dependent:` cleanup, re-derived visibility — and notes BatchedRemoveStatusService is critical because it `bypasses all callbacks`. Each tier carries a 'why high vs medium' rationale.

uncertainty.rationale: Implicitly flags scope via sense_blast counts and 'must re-verify' list, but doesn't call out which dependents the static pass is least sure about (e.g., association/concern-reached ones).

relationship.rationale: Ties most at-risk items to the connecting relation (`before_destroy ... prepend: true` ordering, `dependent: :nullify`, default_scope bypass, manual deindex, recent.kept + visibility scopes) rather than a bare ranked list; a few medium-tier items remain named without an explicit route.

map_quality.rationale: Delivers a complete actionable map: model + concerns to edit with file:line, every dependent area grouped (teardown/distribution/federation/feeds/moderation/counters/search), and existing+to-add specs with fabricators. Embedded JSON consolidates Steps 1-7 so the agent can land the change.

specificity.rationale: Concrete throughout — `app/models/status.rb:34`, `concerns/status/safe_reblog_insert.rb`, `spec/models/concerns/status/{fasp_concern,...}_spec.rb`, explicit new specs (`visibility_spec.rb`, `safe_reblog_insert_spec.rb`) and fabricator paths.

justification.rationale: Specs-to-add lines tie reasons to behaviors ("cover discard cascade, reblog-of-discarded insert rejection, conversation unlink, media nullify/orphan, search deindex"), but several edit targets (snapshot/fasp/search concerns) are listed without a per-file why.

uncertainty.rationale: Flags missing visibility/safe_reblog_insert/snapshot concern specs ("no `visibility`/`safe_reblog_insert` concern spec present") and notes the graph-vs-blast count reconciliation, but doesn't explicitly mark which map pieces it is least sure about.

relationship.rationale: Connects each edit to its role (concern supplies visibility storage, SafeReblogInsert is the discard-aware insert, teardown methods at specific lines), and the embedded JSON's step5_guards/step6_blast_radius wire dependents to the relation that makes them risky; a few spec entries are listed without an explicit relation chain.
